title: Mortgage Loan Originator
description: Sources and processes residential mortgage applications, assessing borrower eligibility and coordinating with underwriting teams to fund loans destined for securitisation pools.
key_tasks: Source and qualify mortgage applicants
Collect and verify borrower documentation
Coordinate with underwriters and valuers
Ensure loans meet pool eligibility criteria
Manage pipeline and conversion rates
ai_exposure_rationale: Much of the documentation gathering, eligibility checking, and data entry is highly automatable. However, client relationship management and advisory still require human interaction.
automation_risk_rationale: Digital mortgage platforms and AI-driven pre-qualification are reducing headcount needs. Roles increasingly shift to oversight of automated pipelines rather than manual processing.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Subject to FCA/PRA mortgage conduct rules, EU Mortgage Credit Directive, and consumer protection regulations. Responsible lending assessments are heavily regulated.

title: Consumer Loan Underwriter
description: Evaluates creditworthiness of consumer loan applicants (auto, personal, credit cards) using credit scoring models and policy rules, deciding on loan approval for assets that may be securitised.
key_tasks: Assess borrower creditworthiness using scoring models
Apply lending policy rules and exceptions
Review and validate applicant documentation
Make approve/decline/refer decisions
Ensure compliance with responsible lending standards
ai_exposure_rationale: Credit decisioning is increasingly automated through ML scoring models and rules engines. The core analytical task of risk assessment is highly susceptible to AI automation.
automation_risk_rationale: Automated credit decisioning already handles the majority of straightforward applications. Human underwriters increasingly handle only exceptions and complex cases, reducing overall headcount.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Must comply with Consumer Credit Act, FCA CONC rules, EU Consumer Credit Directive, and anti-discrimination regulations in lending decisions.

title: Auto Loan Originator
description: Manages the origination of auto finance loans through dealer networks and direct channels, ensuring loan quality standards for auto ABS securitisation programmes.
key_tasks: Manage dealer finance relationships
Process auto loan applications
Verify vehicle valuations and documentation
Monitor loan quality metrics for ABS eligibility
Coordinate with securitisation teams on pool selection
ai_exposure_rationale: Application processing and documentation verification are highly automatable. Dealer relationship management provides some protection but digital dealer platforms are growing.
automation_risk_rationale: Point-of-sale finance platforms increasingly automate the origination process. However, dealer network management and complex deal negotiation still require human involvement.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Subject to consumer credit regulations, FCA rules on motor finance commission disclosure, and EU/UK consumer protection frameworks.

title: Commercial Loan Officer
description: Originates commercial and corporate loans (CRE, leveraged, trade finance) that feed into CLO, CMBS, and other structured finance vehicles.
key_tasks: Source and structure commercial loan transactions
Conduct financial analysis of corporate borrowers
Negotiate loan terms and covenants
Coordinate with credit committees and legal
Monitor portfolio performance and renewals
ai_exposure_rationale: While financial analysis and documentation can be AI-assisted, the core of commercial lending involves relationship management, complex negotiation, and bespoke deal structuring that requires significant human judgment.
automation_risk_rationale: Commercial lending is relationship-intensive with bespoke deal structures. AI will augment productivity but is unlikely to significantly reduce headcount in the near term.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Subject to Basel III capital requirements, PRA large exposure rules, and leveraged lending guidelines. Less consumer-focused regulation but significant prudential oversight.

title: Warehouse Lending Analyst
description: Manages warehouse credit facilities that fund loan origination prior to securitisation, monitoring collateral, advance rates, and facility utilisation.
key_tasks: Monitor warehouse facility utilisation and limits
Calculate and verify advance rates and margin calls
Reconcile collateral positions daily
Prepare facility compliance reports
Coordinate loan takeout into securitisation vehicles
ai_exposure_rationale: The role is predominantly data-driven with routine calculations, reconciliations, and monitoring tasks. These are highly susceptible to automation through AI and rule-based systems.
automation_risk_rationale: Most daily tasks (reconciliation, limit monitoring, advance rate calculation) can be fully automated. The role may consolidate significantly as platforms mature.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Warehouse facilities are bilateral arrangements with less direct regulatory prescription, though subject to general banking prudential requirements and margin rules.

title: Structured Finance Analyst
description: Builds and maintains cash flow waterfall models for ABS/MBS/CLO transactions, analysing deal structures, tranching, credit enhancement levels, and prepayment assumptions.
key_tasks: Build cash flow waterfall models in Excel/Python
Analyse credit enhancement and subordination levels
Model prepayment, default, and recovery scenarios
Prepare investor presentations and deal summaries
Monitor portfolio performance metrics post-issuance
ai_exposure_rationale: Core work is digital modelling and data analysis. AI can increasingly generate waterfall models, run scenario analyses, and produce deal summaries. However, structuring creativity and deal judgment still require human expertise.
automation_risk_rationale: While AI will transform the tools used, the role requires deal-specific judgment, structuring creativity, and investor interaction that resist full automation. Headcount may compress but not disappear.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Must comply with EU STS Regulation, UK Securitisation Regulation, risk retention rules (Article 6 EU SR), and transparency requirements under CRA3.

title: Quantitative Analyst
description: Develops quantitative models for pricing, risk measurement, and portfolio analytics in structured credit products including prepayment models, default correlations, and copula frameworks.
key_tasks: Develop prepayment and default models
Build pricing models for structured products
Validate and back-test existing models
Research new quantitative methodologies
Implement models in Python/C++ production systems
ai_exposure_rationale: AI is transforming quantitative finance with ML-based models replacing traditional approaches. However, model design, validation, and interpretation require deep expertise that AI augments rather than replaces.
automation_risk_rationale: Quant roles require novel research, model innovation, and deep domain expertise. AI tools make quants more productive but the creative and research aspects resist automation.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Models must meet PRA/EBA model risk management standards (SS1/23), Basel III IRB requirements, and IFRS 9 ECL modelling guidelines.

title: Financial Engineer
description: Designs and optimises the capital structure of securitisation transactions, determining optimal tranching, credit enhancement, and structural features to achieve target ratings and investor demand.
key_tasks: Design optimal capital structures for ABS/MBS/CLO
Determine tranching and credit enhancement levels
Optimise structures for rating agency methodologies
Analyse regulatory capital implications
Evaluate structural features (triggers, waterfalls, reserves)
ai_exposure_rationale: Structural design involves significant creativity and market judgment. While AI can assist with scenario analysis and optimisation, the bespoke nature of deal structuring limits full automation.
automation_risk_rationale: Deal structuring is inherently bespoke, requiring negotiation with investors and rating agencies. This relationship and judgment-intensive work resists automation.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Directly shaped by EU STS criteria, risk retention rules, Basel III securitisation framework (SEC-IRBA/SEC-SA), and Solvency II capital charges for investors.

title: Deal Structurer
description: Leads the end-to-end structuring of securitisation transactions, coordinating between origination, legal, rating agencies, and investors to bring deals to market.
key_tasks: Lead deal structuring from mandate to closing
Coordinate rating agency presentations and dialogue
Manage investor roadshows and feedback
Negotiate structural terms with all parties
Oversee legal documentation and closing process
ai_exposure_rationale: Primarily a coordination and negotiation role requiring deep market knowledge and relationship management. AI assists with analysis and documentation but the core of the role is human-centric.
automation_risk_rationale: Deal structuring requires bespoke negotiation, market judgment, and multi-party coordination. This is one of the most relationship-intensive roles in securitisation.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Must navigate EU STS framework, risk retention, disclosure requirements, prospectus regulation, and rating agency regulatory frameworks simultaneously.

title: Cash Flow Modeller
description: Specialises in building and maintaining detailed cash flow waterfall models that simulate payment distributions across tranches under various economic scenarios.
key_tasks: Build and maintain waterfall payment models
Implement complex priority of payments logic
Run stress tests and sensitivity analyses
Validate models against trustee reports
Document model assumptions and methodologies
ai_exposure_rationale: Cash flow modelling is a highly structured, rule-based digital task. AI can generate waterfall logic, run scenarios, and validate outputs with increasing accuracy. This is one of the most automatable roles in structuring.
automation_risk_rationale: As AI becomes capable of generating and validating waterfall models directly from legal documentation, the need for dedicated cash flow modellers will decrease significantly.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Models must reflect regulatory waterfall requirements and STS criteria, but the role itself is more technical than regulatory.

title: Tranching Specialist
description: Analyses and optimises the credit tranching of securitisation vehicles, determining attachment/detachment points, subordination levels, and credit enhancement to achieve desired rating outcomes.
key_tasks: Determine optimal attachment and detachment points
Analyse subordination and credit enhancement levels
Model expected loss distributions across tranches
Interact with rating agencies on tranche analysis
Optimise capital structure for investor demand and regulation
ai_exposure_rationale: Tranching analysis involves significant quantitative work that AI can accelerate. However, optimising for multiple objectives (ratings, regulation, investor demand) requires market judgment.
automation_risk_rationale: While analytical components are automatable, the strategic decisions around tranching involve rating agency dialogue and market positioning that require human judgment.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Directly governed by Basel III securitisation framework capital treatment, EU STS criteria for tranching, and CRR requirements for significant risk transfer.

title: Securitisation Lawyer
description: Advises on legal structuring of securitisation transactions, drafts transaction documentation, and provides opinions on true sale, bankruptcy remoteness, and regulatory compliance.
key_tasks: Draft and negotiate transaction documents (PPMs, indentures)
Provide true sale and non-consolidation opinions
Advise on regulatory compliance (EU STS, UK SR)
Structure SPVs and bankruptcy-remote vehicles
Coordinate with multiple counsel across jurisdictions
ai_exposure_rationale: AI is increasingly capable of drafting and reviewing standard securitisation documentation. However, bespoke structuring advice, multi-jurisdictional legal analysis, and negotiation remain human-intensive.
automation_risk_rationale: Legal advisory requires professional judgment, client relationships, and liability acceptance that cannot be delegated to AI. Junior drafting roles face more risk than senior advisory.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Must navigate complex intersection of securitisation regulation, banking law, insolvency law, tax law, securities regulation, and data protection across multiple EU/UK jurisdictions.

title: Documentation Specialist
description: Manages the preparation, review, and maintenance of securitisation transaction documentation including prospectuses, servicing agreements, and investor reports.
key_tasks: Prepare and proofread transaction documents
Manage document version control and execution
Coordinate document review across parties
Maintain document templates and precedent libraries
Ensure disclosure compliance in offering documents
ai_exposure_rationale: Document preparation, review, version control, and template management are highly repetitive digital tasks that AI can largely automate. LLMs can draft, compare, and check documents with increasing accuracy.
automation_risk_rationale: As AI document generation and review tools mature, the need for dedicated documentation specialists will decrease substantially. This is among the most at-risk roles.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Must ensure documents comply with prospectus regulation, EU STS disclosure requirements, and investor transparency standards, but relies on legal counsel for interpretation.

title: Closing Paralegal
description: Coordinates the closing process for securitisation transactions, managing signature pages, conditions precedent checklists, and post-closing deliverables.
key_tasks: Manage closing checklists and conditions precedent
Coordinate signature execution across parties
Prepare closing binders and post-closing deliverables
Track and chase outstanding closing items
Maintain closing calendars and timelines
ai_exposure_rationale: Closing coordination is largely administrative and process-driven. AI can automate checklist tracking, document assembly, and status monitoring. The remaining value is in managing human coordination.
automation_risk_rationale: Smart contract and workflow automation platforms are increasingly handling closing processes. The role will likely consolidate into broader legal operations functions.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: The closing process itself has limited direct regulatory requirements beyond ensuring all regulatory conditions are met, which is typically directed by lawyers.

title: Regulatory Counsel
description: Provides specialist legal advice on the regulatory framework governing securitisation, including EU STS Regulation, UK Securitisation Regulation, CRR, and related prudential requirements.
key_tasks: Advise on EU STS and UK Securitisation Regulation compliance
Interpret new regulatory requirements and guidance
Prepare regulatory applications and notifications
Liaise with regulators (FCA, PRA, EBA, ESMA)
Train business teams on regulatory developments
ai_exposure_rationale: While AI can assist with regulatory research and monitoring, interpreting novel regulatory requirements and advising on their application to specific transactions requires deep professional judgment.
automation_risk_rationale: Regulatory interpretation is inherently judgment-based and constantly evolving. Demand for regulatory counsel tends to increase with regulatory complexity rather than decrease.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: This role exists entirely because of regulation. The raison d'être is navigating the complex, multi-layered regulatory framework governing securitisation.

title: True Sale Opinion Counsel
description: Provides legal opinions on the true sale characterisation of asset transfers in securitisation, ensuring bankruptcy remoteness of SPV structures.
key_tasks: Analyse true sale vs secured lending characterisation
Draft true sale and non-consolidation legal opinions
Review SPV structural features for bankruptcy remoteness
Advise on perfection of security interests
Monitor case law developments affecting true sale analysis
ai_exposure_rationale: Legal opinion work involves standardised analysis but requires professional judgment and acceptance of legal liability. AI can assist research but the opinion itself requires human accountability.
automation_risk_rationale: Legal opinions carry professional liability and require bar-qualified signatories. This inherently limits automation, though AI will assist in the analytical process.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: True sale analysis intersects with insolvency law, property law, and securitisation regulation. EU STS requirements add specific structural requirements for bankruptcy remoteness.

title: Rating Agency Analyst
description: Analyses securitisation transactions for credit rating agencies, applying rating methodologies to assign and monitor ratings on structured finance tranches.
key_tasks: Apply rating methodologies to new securitisation deals
Build and run rating agency cash flow models
Present rating recommendations to rating committees
Conduct annual surveillance reviews
Publish presale reports and rating rationales
ai_exposure_rationale: Rating analysis is heavily model-driven and methodology-based, making it susceptible to AI automation. However, rating committee judgment and investor communication provide some protection.
automation_risk_rationale: While analytical work can be automated, rating agencies' oligopolistic position and regulatory mandate protect overall headcount. The role will transform rather than disappear.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Rating agencies are regulated under CRA Regulation (EU), with ESMA oversight. Analysts must follow approved methodologies and comply with conflict-of-interest rules.

title: Credit Risk Modeller
description: Develops and maintains credit risk models for securitised portfolios, including PD/LGD/EAD models, stress testing frameworks, and portfolio credit risk analytics.
key_tasks: Develop PD, LGD, and EAD models for loan portfolios
Build stress testing and scenario analysis frameworks
Validate and back-test credit risk models
Calibrate models to through-the-cycle and point-in-time metrics
Support regulatory capital calculations (IRB approach)
ai_exposure_rationale: Credit risk modelling is increasingly leveraging ML techniques. AI can automate model development, validation, and calibration. However, model design decisions and regulatory interpretation require expert judgment.
automation_risk_rationale: Regulatory requirements for model governance, validation, and explainability create ongoing demand for human modellers. ML may change the tools but not eliminate the role.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Models must comply with CRR IRB requirements, EBA Guidelines on PD/LGD estimation, IFRS 9 ECL standards, and PRA model risk management expectations (SS1/23).

title: Surveillance Analyst
description: Monitors the ongoing performance of securitised portfolios, tracking collateral performance, trigger breaches, and structural features to identify credit deterioration.
key_tasks: Monitor collateral performance metrics (delinquency, default, prepayment)
Track structural trigger levels and covenant compliance
Prepare periodic surveillance reports for investors
Identify and escalate credit deterioration signals
Maintain performance databases and dashboards
ai_exposure_rationale: Surveillance is heavily data-driven and rule-based. AI can automate performance monitoring, trigger detection, anomaly identification, and report generation with high accuracy.
automation_risk_rationale: Automated monitoring platforms can handle most routine surveillance. Human oversight will be needed for complex situations but at significantly reduced headcount.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: EU STS requires ongoing monitoring and reporting. CRA Regulation mandates surveillance processes for rating agencies. Article 7 EU SR requires investor reporting standards.

title: RMBS/CMBS Analyst
description: Specialises in analysing residential and commercial mortgage-backed securities, evaluating property markets, loan-level data, and structural features specific to real estate securitisation.
key_tasks: Analyse loan-level mortgage data for RMBS/CMBS pools
Evaluate commercial property valuations and cash flows
Model property market scenarios and stress tests
Assess loan-to-value distributions and concentration risk
Monitor real estate market trends and their impact on portfolios
ai_exposure_rationale: Data analysis and modelling components are highly automatable. However, property market judgment, site-specific knowledge, and qualitative assessment of borrower/sponsor quality require human expertise.
automation_risk_rationale: Real estate assessment combines quantitative analysis with qualitative market judgment. While AI enhances analytical capacity, the need for property-specific expertise limits headcount reduction.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: RMBS/CMBS must comply with EU STS requirements specific to real estate, EBA data templates, and national property law variations across EU member states.

title: Loss-Given-Default Modeller
description: Develops specialised models estimating recovery rates and loss severity for defaulted loans in securitised portfolios, incorporating collateral valuations, workout timelines, and enforcement costs.
key_tasks: Build LGD models incorporating collateral and recovery data
Analyse workout and enforcement cost distributions
Model recovery timing and discounting effects
Calibrate models across asset classes and jurisdictions
Validate LGD estimates against actual recovery outcomes
ai_exposure_rationale: LGD modelling is highly quantitative and data-intensive. AI/ML approaches can improve prediction accuracy and automate model development. The role is predominantly digital and analytical.
automation_risk_rationale: While model development can be increasingly automated, regulatory requirements for model governance and the need for jurisdiction-specific expertise maintain demand for specialists.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: LGD models must comply with CRR IRB downturn LGD requirements, EBA Guidelines on LGD estimation, and regulatory expectations for through-the-cycle calibration.

title: Compliance Officer
description: Ensures securitisation activities comply with applicable regulations including EU STS, CRR, and national implementation. Manages regulatory relationships and compliance monitoring programmes.
key_tasks: Monitor compliance with securitisation regulations
Develop and maintain compliance policies and procedures
Conduct compliance reviews and testing
Report to senior management and regulators
Train front office on regulatory requirements
ai_exposure_rationale: Compliance monitoring, testing, and reporting are increasingly automatable with RegTech tools. However, regulatory interpretation, judgment calls on grey areas, and stakeholder management remain human-intensive.
automation_risk_rationale: Growing regulatory complexity tends to increase demand for compliance roles. While AI tools improve efficiency, the accountability and judgment aspects resist full automation.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: The role's entire purpose is regulatory compliance. Must navigate EU Securitisation Regulation, CRR, MiFID II, MAR, EMIR, and national variations across multiple jurisdictions.

title: Regulatory Reporting Analyst
description: Prepares and submits regulatory reports related to securitisation exposures, including COREP, securitisation notifications, and STS verification reports to national competent authorities.
key_tasks: Prepare COREP securitisation exposure reports
Submit STS notifications to ESMA/FCA
Compile Article 7 EU SR transparency reports
Reconcile regulatory data with internal systems
Respond to regulatory data quality queries
ai_exposure_rationale: Regulatory reporting is highly structured, rule-based, and data-driven. AI can automate data extraction, transformation, validation, and report generation with high accuracy.
automation_risk_rationale: RegTech platforms are increasingly automating the end-to-end reporting process. Dedicated reporting analyst roles will consolidate significantly as automation matures.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Must comply with EBA reporting standards (ITS on reporting), ESMA STS notification requirements, and national regulator-specific submission formats and timelines.

title: AML/KYC Specialist
description: Conducts anti-money laundering and know-your-customer due diligence on securitisation counterparties, including originators, servicers, investors, and SPV directors.
key_tasks: Conduct KYC due diligence on counterparties
Screen parties against sanctions and PEP lists
Investigate suspicious transaction alerts
Maintain and refresh KYC records periodically
File suspicious activity reports (SARs)
ai_exposure_rationale: KYC data gathering, screening, and routine due diligence are highly automatable. AI can process documents, match entities, and flag anomalies more efficiently than manual review.
automation_risk_rationale: Automated KYC platforms and AI screening tools are rapidly reducing the need for manual analysts. However, complex investigations and judgment calls on elevated risk cases still require humans.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Governed by EU Anti-Money Laundering Directives (AMLD), UK Money Laundering Regulations, sanctions regulations, and regulatory expectations on customer due diligence.

title: Basel/RWA Analyst
description: Calculates and analyses risk-weighted assets for securitisation exposures under Basel III framework, supporting capital adequacy reporting and optimisation.
key_tasks: Calculate RWA for securitisation exposures (SEC-IRBA, SEC-SA, SEC-ERBA)
Analyse significant risk transfer (SRT) test outcomes
Support regulatory capital optimisation strategies
Prepare capital adequacy reports and analysis
Monitor regulatory capital developments and impact
ai_exposure_rationale: RWA calculation is rule-based and highly structured. AI can automate calculations, run sensitivity analyses, and generate reports. However, interpreting regulatory rules and optimising structures requires expertise.
automation_risk_rationale: While calculations are automatable, the evolving Basel framework and need for regulatory interpretation maintain demand for specialists. The role may evolve into more advisory functions.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Entirely driven by Basel III/CRR securitisation framework. The role exists solely because of regulatory capital requirements and would not exist without prudential regulation.

title: Risk & Controls Manager
description: Manages the operational risk framework and internal controls for securitisation activities, including control testing, risk event management, and regulatory exam preparation.
key_tasks: Design and maintain internal control frameworks
Conduct risk and control self-assessments (RCSAs)
Manage operational risk events and losses
Prepare for and support regulatory examinations
Report to risk committees on control effectiveness
ai_exposure_rationale: Control design and risk assessment involve significant judgment and stakeholder management. While AI can assist with control testing and monitoring, the strategic and interpersonal aspects are less automatable.
automation_risk_rationale: Risk management is increasingly valued and regulated. Demand for risk professionals tends to grow with regulatory expectations rather than decrease with automation.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Governed by PRA operational resilience requirements, EBA Guidelines on internal governance, and Basel operational risk framework. Subject to increasing regulatory scrutiny.

title: Master Servicer
description: Oversees the servicing of securitised loan portfolios, ensuring primary servicers comply with servicing standards, collecting performance data, and managing investor reporting.
key_tasks: Oversee primary servicer performance and compliance
Collect and validate monthly performance data
Prepare investor reports and trustee notifications
Monitor servicing standards and SLAs
Manage servicer transitions and contingency planning
ai_exposure_rationale: Data collection, validation, and reporting are highly automatable. However, servicer relationship management, exception handling, and contingency planning require human judgment.
automation_risk_rationale: Automated servicing platforms reduce manual work, but the oversight and governance function remains important. The role will become more strategic and less operational.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Subject to EU STS servicing standards, FCA MCOB rules for mortgage servicing, and contractual servicing obligations embedded in transaction documentation.

title: Special Servicer
description: Manages defaulted or distressed loans within securitised portfolios, executing workout strategies including modifications, forbearance, enforcement, and asset disposal.
key_tasks: Develop and execute workout strategies for defaulted loans
Negotiate loan modifications and forbearance arrangements
Manage enforcement proceedings and asset disposals
Maximise recovery values for investors
Report workout progress to controlling parties
ai_exposure_rationale: Workout management requires significant negotiation skill, debtor interaction, and judgment on complex situations. While AI can assist with analytics, the human element is central to effective workouts.
automation_risk_rationale: Special servicing is inherently bespoke and relationship-intensive. Each defaulted loan requires tailored strategies and human negotiation. Automation potential is limited.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Must comply with FCA debt collection rules, consumer protection regulations, and EU/UK requirements for fair treatment of borrowers in difficulty.

title: Collections Specialist
description: Manages the collection of overdue payments from borrowers in securitised portfolios, using outbound contact strategies and payment arrangement negotiations.
key_tasks: Contact delinquent borrowers via phone, email, and letter
Negotiate payment arrangements and catch-up plans
Apply collections policies and escalation procedures
Update account records and payment status
Meet collection targets and performance metrics
ai_exposure_rationale: Outbound contact, payment arrangement calculation, and account management are increasingly automated through chatbots, AI dialers, and digital payment platforms.
automation_risk_rationale: AI chatbots and automated communication are replacing routine collections contacts. The role is shrinking as digital self-service payment tools reduce the need for human agents.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Subject to FCA CONC rules on debt collection, consumer protection regulations, and requirements for fair treatment of customers in financial difficulty.

title: Escrow Analyst
description: Manages escrow accounts associated with securitised mortgage loans, ensuring accurate collection, administration, and disbursement of insurance and tax payments.
key_tasks: Calculate and adjust escrow payment amounts
Process insurance and tax disbursements
Conduct annual escrow analyses and shortage/surplus adjustments
Reconcile escrow balances with third-party data
Respond to borrower escrow inquiries
ai_exposure_rationale: Escrow administration is highly structured, rule-based, and data-driven. Calculations, reconciliations, and adjustments are almost entirely automatable.
automation_risk_rationale: Automated escrow platforms can handle nearly all routine escrow functions. This role is among the most vulnerable to full automation in servicing.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Subject to general mortgage servicing regulations but escrow administration itself has relatively straightforward regulatory requirements.

title: Default Management Specialist
description: Manages the default process for securitised loans from early delinquency through to resolution, coordinating between collections, legal, and workout teams.
key_tasks: Manage default timelines and regulatory requirements
Coordinate between collections, legal, and special servicing
Assess loss mitigation options and eligibility
Track default resolution outcomes and report metrics
Ensure compliance with pre-action protocols
ai_exposure_rationale: Process management, timeline tracking, and option assessment are automatable. However, coordinating across teams and making judgment calls on loss mitigation require human oversight.
automation_risk_rationale: Workflow automation can handle routine default processing, but complex cases and multi-party coordination maintain the need for human specialists at reduced headcount.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Must comply with FCA mortgage arrears rules, pre-action protocol requirements, MCOB 13 forbearance guidelines, and consumer protection obligations.

title: ABS Trader
description: Trades asset-backed securities in the secondary market, managing inventory positions, providing liquidity to clients, and pricing new issue allocations.
key_tasks: Price and trade ABS in secondary markets
Manage trading book positions and risk limits
Provide liquidity and market-making to clients
Participate in new issue pricing and allocation
Monitor market conditions and relative value
ai_exposure_rationale: While AI can assist with pricing analytics and market monitoring, ABS trading involves illiquid markets requiring relationship-based execution, market judgment, and client interaction that resist full automation.
automation_risk_rationale: Structured credit markets remain relatively illiquid and OTC. Unlike equities, full electronic trading automation is limited by market structure and the bespoke nature of securities.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Subject to MiFID II/MiFIR reporting, MAR market abuse rules, CRR market risk capital requirements, and EMIR derivatives clearing obligations for related hedges.

title: MBS Trader
description: Specialises in trading mortgage-backed securities and covered bonds, managing prepayment risk, duration, and convexity in the trading portfolio.
key_tasks: Trade MBS and covered bonds in secondary markets
Manage prepayment and interest rate risk
Analyse relative value across MBS sectors
Hedge duration and convexity exposures
Price new issue covered bond and RMBS deals
ai_exposure_rationale: MBS trading requires understanding complex prepayment dynamics and market microstructure. AI enhances analytics but the judgment needed for illiquid market trading limits full automation.
automation_risk_rationale: Covered bonds have more electronic trading, but RMBS remains OTC and relationship-driven. Full automation is constrained by market structure and product complexity.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Subject to MiFID II, MAR, EMIR for hedging derivatives, covered bond regulation (EU Covered Bond Directive), and CRR market risk requirements.

title: CLO Trader
description: Trades collateralised loan obligation tranches and leveraged loans, managing credit risk positions and providing pricing for institutional investors.
key_tasks: Price and trade CLO tranches (AAA to equity)
Analyse CLO manager performance and deal structures
Monitor leveraged loan market fundamentals
Manage credit and market risk positions
Provide liquidity to institutional investor clients
ai_exposure_rationale: CLO markets are relationship-intensive and illiquid. While AI improves analytics and pricing models, the market structure and bespoke nature of CLO tranches limit full automation.
automation_risk_rationale: CLO trading requires deep understanding of deal structures, manager behaviour, and leveraged loan market dynamics. The highly specialised knowledge and thin market limit automation potential.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Subject to EU/UK securitisation regulation risk retention, CRR capital requirements, MiFID II, and increasing regulatory scrutiny on leveraged lending exposure.

title: Structured Credit Sales
description: Markets securitisation products and structured credit to institutional investors, providing trade ideas, market colour, and new issue distribution.
key_tasks: Develop and maintain institutional investor relationships
Distribute new issue ABS/MBS/CLO to investors
Provide market commentary and trade ideas
Coordinate between traders and investor clients
Organise investor roadshows and conferences
ai_exposure_rationale: Sales is fundamentally relationship-driven. While AI can generate market commentary and trade ideas, the core value is in client relationships, trust, and understanding investor mandates.
automation_risk_rationale: Institutional sales in illiquid markets is among the most relationship-dependent roles. High-touch client interaction and bespoke advisory resist automation.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Must comply with MiFID II suitability and appropriateness requirements, product governance rules, and inducements regulations when distributing structured products.

title: Portfolio Manager - Structured Credit
description: Manages investment portfolios allocated to securitisation and structured credit, making buy/sell decisions, and reporting to institutional investors.
key_tasks: Make investment decisions across ABS/MBS/CLO sectors
Conduct fundamental credit and relative value analysis
Manage portfolio risk, duration, and credit exposure
Report portfolio performance to investors
Develop investment theses and sector views
ai_exposure_rationale: Portfolio management combines quantitative analysis with qualitative judgment and market intuition. AI enhances analytics but investment decision-making requires holistic judgment that resists full automation.
automation_risk_rationale: While robo-advisory exists in simpler markets, structured credit portfolio management requires deep expertise and judgment. Fiduciary responsibility also limits delegation to AI.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Subject to AIFMD/UCITS requirements, EU Securitisation Regulation due diligence obligations (Article 5), and MiFID II portfolio management rules.

title: Repo Desk Analyst
description: Manages repo and securities financing transactions using securitisation products as collateral, optimising funding costs and balance sheet efficiency.
key_tasks: Execute repo and reverse repo transactions
Manage collateral eligibility and haircut schedules
Optimise funding costs across repo counterparties
Monitor margin calls and collateral movements
Calculate and report SFT exposures
ai_exposure_rationale: Repo operations involve significant routine processing, collateral management, and optimisation that are increasingly automated. However, counterparty negotiations and market judgment provide some protection.
automation_risk_rationale: Electronic repo platforms and automated collateral management are reducing manual involvement. However, bespoke repo terms and relationship management maintain some human involvement.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Subject to SFTR reporting requirements, EMIR margining rules, Basel III leverage ratio and LCR treatment, and ECB eligibility criteria for central bank repo.

title: Fintech Developer
description: Develops software platforms and applications for securitisation workflows, including loan origination systems, deal management platforms, and investor portals.
key_tasks: Develop securitisation workflow platforms
Build loan origination and servicing systems
Create investor reporting portals and APIs
Implement data pipelines for loan-level analytics
Integrate with third-party services (rating agencies, trustees)
ai_exposure_rationale: Software development is heavily impacted by AI coding assistants. AI can generate, review, and debug code with increasing capability. However, system architecture and domain understanding still require human expertise.
automation_risk_rationale: AI makes developers more productive but demand for custom securitisation technology is growing. The net effect is likely transformation rather than headcount reduction.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Must ensure systems comply with data protection (GDPR), operational resilience requirements (PRA), and that platforms support regulatory reporting and audit trail requirements.

title: Data Engineer
description: Designs and maintains data infrastructure for securitisation analytics, including loan-level data warehouses, ETL pipelines, and data quality frameworks.
key_tasks: Design loan-level data warehouse architectures
Build ETL pipelines for loan tape processing
Implement data quality and validation frameworks
Manage European DataWarehouse (ED) submissions
Optimise data infrastructure for analytics and reporting
ai_exposure_rationale: Data engineering involves significant coding and pipeline development that AI can assist with. However, understanding securitisation data models and business requirements requires domain expertise.
automation_risk_rationale: Growing data requirements (ED templates, ESMA reporting) increase demand. AI tools improve productivity but the expanding data landscape maintains the need for specialists.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Must comply with EBA/ESMA loan-level data templates, European DataWarehouse requirements, GDPR data protection, and regulatory data quality standards.

title: Risk Systems Developer
description: Develops and maintains risk management systems for securitisation, including credit risk engines, capital calculation tools, and stress testing platforms.
key_tasks: Develop credit risk calculation engines
Build regulatory capital computation systems
Implement stress testing and scenario analysis platforms
Maintain model libraries and risk databases
Integrate risk systems with front office and reporting
ai_exposure_rationale: Risk system development combines software engineering with quantitative finance expertise. AI assists with coding but the domain-specific integration requires specialised knowledge.
automation_risk_rationale: Evolving regulatory requirements constantly create demand for system updates and new capabilities. The specialised intersection of technology and risk management limits automation.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Systems must implement Basel III calculation methodologies, meet PRA model risk standards, and support FRTB and IFRS 9 requirements with full audit trails.

title: Loan Platform Engineer
description: Builds and maintains digital loan origination and management platforms that produce loans suitable for securitisation, ensuring data capture meets pool eligibility and regulatory standards.
key_tasks: Develop digital loan origination workflows
Implement credit decisioning engine integrations
Ensure loan data capture meets securitisation eligibility
Build APIs for data exchange with servicers and trustees
Maintain platform scalability and performance
ai_exposure_rationale: Platform engineering is heavily impacted by AI coding tools. However, understanding the specific requirements of securitisation-eligible loan data capture requires domain expertise.
automation_risk_rationale: Growing digitisation of lending increases demand for platform engineers. AI makes them more productive but the expanding scope of digital lending platforms maintains headcount.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Platforms must comply with consumer credit regulations, ensure data capture meets EBA loan-level templates, and support regulatory reporting requirements.

title: RegTech Developer
description: Develops regulatory technology solutions specifically for securitisation compliance, including STS verification tools, regulatory reporting engines, and compliance monitoring platforms.
key_tasks: Build STS compliance verification tools
Develop automated regulatory reporting engines
Create compliance monitoring dashboards
Implement rule engines for regulatory checks
Maintain regulatory change management systems
ai_exposure_rationale: RegTech development involves translating regulatory rules into code, which AI can assist with. However, accurately interpreting regulatory requirements and edge cases requires human expertise.
automation_risk_rationale: Demand for RegTech solutions is growing rapidly as regulation increases. These developers are building the automation tools rather than being replaced by them.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Must deeply understand the regulations they are encoding: EU STS criteria, CRR capital calculations, ESMA reporting standards, and cross-jurisdictional regulatory variations.

title: Due Diligence Analyst
description: Conducts due diligence reviews on securitised loan portfolios, verifying loan data accuracy, compliance with eligibility criteria, and adherence to origination standards.
key_tasks: Review loan files for data accuracy and completeness
Verify compliance with pool eligibility criteria
Assess adherence to origination policies and standards
Identify data quality issues and exceptions
Prepare due diligence reports and exception summaries
ai_exposure_rationale: Loan file review is a highly structured, document-intensive process. AI can read, extract, and verify loan data against criteria with increasing speed and accuracy, making this one of the most automatable roles.
automation_risk_rationale: AI document review and data extraction tools are rapidly replacing manual loan file review. Headcount in due diligence is already declining as platforms automate the review process.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Due diligence requirements are driven by EU Securitisation Regulation Article 5 investor obligations and Article 9 originator requirements, plus STS verification standards.

title: Third-Party Reviewer
description: Provides independent third-party verification of securitised loan pools for STS compliance, conducting sample-based reviews of loan eligibility and data quality.
key_tasks: Conduct independent sample-based loan reviews
Verify STS eligibility criteria compliance
Assess originator underwriting standards
Provide independent verification reports
Maintain quality standards across review teams
ai_exposure_rationale: Third-party review is largely a data verification exercise that AI can perform efficiently. However, the independent assurance function and regulatory standing of the reviewer provide some protection.
automation_risk_rationale: While the verification process itself is automatable, the regulatory requirement for independent third-party review (STS Article 28) maintains demand for the function if not the current headcount.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Directly mandated by EU STS Regulation Article 28 requirements for third-party verification. Must follow EBA standards on STS verification criteria.

title: Internal Auditor - Securitisation
description: Conducts internal audit reviews of securitisation activities, assessing compliance with policies, regulations, and control effectiveness across the securitisation value chain.
key_tasks: Plan and execute audit reviews of securitisation activities
Assess control design and operating effectiveness
Test compliance with regulations and internal policies
Report audit findings and recommendations
Track remediation of audit issues
ai_exposure_rationale: Audit testing and data analysis are increasingly automatable. However, audit judgment, finding articulation, and stakeholder engagement require human skills.
automation_risk_rationale: Regulatory expectations for internal audit functions are increasing. While AI tools improve efficiency, the governance role of internal audit maintains demand for experienced professionals.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Must audit compliance with the full spectrum of securitisation regulations. Subject to IIA standards, PRA expectations for internal audit (SS2/21), and CRD requirements.

title: Forensic Loan Reviewer
description: Investigates potential fraud, misrepresentation, and origination defects in securitised loan portfolios, supporting repurchase claims and regulatory investigations.
key_tasks: Investigate suspected loan fraud and misrepresentation
Review origination documentation for material defects
Prepare evidence for repurchase and indemnity claims
Support regulatory investigations and enforcement
Develop fraud detection methodologies and patterns
ai_exposure_rationale: AI can identify fraud patterns, anomalies, and inconsistencies in loan data efficiently. However, investigation judgment, evidence preparation, and regulatory interaction require human expertise.
automation_risk_rationale: While AI enhances fraud detection capabilities, the investigative and evidentiary aspects of forensic review require human judgment and accountability.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Investigations may involve fraud reporting obligations, FCA/PRA regulatory requirements, and legal proceedings requiring understanding of evidence standards.

title: Settlements Specialist
description: Manages the settlement and clearing of securitisation transactions, ensuring accurate trade confirmation, payment processing, and custody movements across clearing systems.
key_tasks: Process trade confirmations and settlement instructions
Manage settlement via Euroclear/Clearstream
Investigate and resolve settlement failures
Reconcile positions and cash balances
Coordinate with custodians and clearing houses
ai_exposure_rationale: Settlement processing is highly structured and rule-based. Automated matching, STP systems, and AI-driven exception handling are rapidly reducing manual involvement.
automation_risk_rationale: Straight-through processing rates continue to increase. AI handles exception management and reconciliation breaks, reducing the need for manual settlement specialists.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Subject to CSDR settlement discipline regime, CSD regulations, and clearing house rules, but the regulatory framework is relatively standardised.

title: Trustee Operations Analyst
description: Manages operational functions for securitisation trustees, including payment waterfalls, covenant compliance monitoring, and investor communications.
key_tasks: Execute waterfall payment calculations and distributions
Monitor transaction covenants and trigger levels
Process investor notices and communications
Maintain transaction records and documentation
Coordinate with servicers on performance data
ai_exposure_rationale: Trustee operations involve highly structured, rule-based tasks: waterfall calculations, covenant monitoring, and payment processing. These are prime candidates for full automation.
automation_risk_rationale: Automated trustee platforms can handle waterfall calculations, covenant monitoring, and payment processing. The role is consolidating rapidly as platforms mature.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Trustee operations are governed more by contractual terms than regulation, though subject to general financial services regulatory requirements and trust law obligations.

title: Investor Reporting Analyst
description: Prepares and distributes periodic investor reports for securitisation transactions, including performance data, pool statistics, and regulatory disclosures.
key_tasks: Prepare monthly/quarterly investor performance reports
Compile pool statistics and collateral performance data
Generate Article 7 EU SR disclosure reports
Submit data to European DataWarehouse and securitisation repositories
Respond to investor data requests and queries
ai_exposure_rationale: Investor reporting is a highly structured, data-driven process. AI can automate data extraction, report generation, template population, and distribution with high accuracy.
automation_risk_rationale: Automated reporting platforms are rapidly replacing manual report preparation. The standardisation of templates (EBA, ED) accelerates automation potential.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Reporting requirements are directly driven by EU Securitisation Regulation Article 7, EBA disclosure templates, ESMA securitisation repositories requirements, and STS ongoing reporting standards.

title: Payment Processing Specialist
description: Manages the processing of principal and interest payments from borrowers through to investors in securitisation structures, ensuring accurate and timely fund flows.
key_tasks: Process borrower payments and allocations
Execute investor payment distributions
Reconcile payment flows across the securitisation chain
Investigate and resolve payment discrepancies
Manage payment processing schedules and deadlines
ai_exposure_rationale: Payment processing is almost entirely rule-based and automatable. Automated payment engines, reconciliation tools, and exception handling significantly reduce manual involvement.
automation_risk_rationale: Straight-through payment processing is already highly automated. Remaining manual roles will continue to be eliminated as AI handles exceptions and reconciliation breaks.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Subject to payment services regulations and operational requirements, but the regulatory framework for payment processing is relatively straightforward compared to other securitisation roles.

title: Fund Accountant - Securitisation
description: Manages the accounting and financial reporting for securitisation vehicles (SPVs), including NAV calculations, IFRS reporting, and tax compliance for structured entities.
key_tasks: Prepare SPV financial statements under IFRS
Calculate and verify NAV for fund vehicles
Manage consolidation and deconsolidation accounting
Process journal entries and accruals for securitisation vehicles
Coordinate with external auditors on SPV audits
ai_exposure_rationale: SPV accounting follows structured IFRS rules and involves routine calculations, journal entries, and reporting. AI can automate the majority of these tasks with appropriate controls.
automation_risk_rationale: While routine accounting is highly automatable, the complexity of securitisation accounting (consolidation, derecognition, IFRS 9) and regulatory scrutiny maintain some demand for specialists.
regulatory_complexity_rationale: Must comply with IFRS 9 (financial instruments), IFRS 10 (consolidation), IAS 39 derecognition criteria, and tax authority requirements for SPV structures across jurisdictions.
